sense.definition: the time when someone has the opportunity, obligation, or right to do a certain thing that everyone in a group does one after the other

examples.x: She took her turn in the game, rolling the dice and moving her game piece.
It was her turn to speak during the meeting, so she stood up and addressed the group.
In the game of Monopoly, players take turns rolling the dice and moving their game pieces around the board.
Each student in the class took turns presenting their projects to the rest of the class.
The siblings took turns doing the dishes after dinner, rotating the chore each night.

sense.definition: the act of removing an opponent's game piece from the board
a change made by a player in the position of a piece in a board game
a designated space on the game board where players can place their game pieces or tokens before they are put into play
a capture when an opponent's game piece is blocked on adjacent sides by two or more of the capturing player's pieces. The blocked piece is then removed from the game board as a result of the capture

examples.x: The player managed a custodian capture by surrounding the opponent’s piece with two of their own.
After several moves, she executed a custodian capture, removing the opponent’s piece from the board.
He set up a strong defense, which eventually led to a custodian capture of his opponent’s piece.
The game took a turn when I made a custodian capture, leaving my opponent with fewer pieces to move.
She didn’t see it coming when I made a custodian capture, trapping her piece on all sides.

sense.definition: a capture method used in some board games where a player's game piece is removed from the board and replaced by the capturing player's piece

examples.x: In the game, he used a displacement capture to take his opponent's piece and move his own into its place.
She was able to use a displacement capture to clear a path for her pieces to advance.
The opponent made a strong move with a displacement capture, leaving me with fewer options.
By using a displacement capture, he not only removed the threat but also strengthened his own position.

sense.definition: a roll of two dice that show the same number
a set of three game pieces or cards or dice that share the same attributes, such as color, shape, or value
a vertical column of squares on a game board
the set of activities, actions, and interactions that take place during the course of a board game or video game

examples.x: The gameplay of this board game is simple but very engaging, making it fun for all ages.
He enjoyed the fast-paced gameplay of the video game, where quick decisions were key to success.
The new version of the game features improved gameplay, with more complex challenges and better graphics.
The game's gameplay involves a mix of strategy and luck, keeping players on their toes.
The competitive gameplay kept the players excited and focused throughout the entire match.

sense.definition: a bonus turn granted to a player that allows them to take an additional action or move beyond what would normally be allowed in a single turn
a set of rules or conditions that are put in place to balance the game and give a disadvantaged player a better chance of winning

the ability of a player to capture an opponent's game piece by removing it from the board when the opponent fails to capture one of the player's pieces that is able to make a capture move on the same turn
used to refer to the cards, pieces, etc. that a player holds and has not yet played

examples.x: I had a few high value cards in hand but wasn't confident they'd beat whatever Sam was holding privately.

sense.definition: the ability of a player to capture an opponent's game piece that has just made a capture move, by moving one of their own pieces to a square adjacent to the captured piece

examples.x: After losing a piece, Sarah used an intervention capture to take one of Tom’s pieces right after he captured hers.
John’s quick thinking allowed him to make an intervention capture, stealing back a piece just as his opponent moved in.
In the next turn, Mark made a brilliant intervention capture, moving his piece next to the opponent's captured piece.
The game turned in Lisa's favor when she performed an intervention capture, taking advantage of Tom’s move.
He knew his opponent was planning an attack, so he was ready to execute an intervention capture if needed.

sense.definition: a rule used to determine who plays first in the game, and to ensure that the starting player does not have an unfair advantage over their opponent

examples.x: At the start of the game, Mark made his first move, but Alice decided to use the pie rule and switched it up.
They played a board game, and after Emma went first, Jake used the pie rule to make the game more balanced.
During a game of checkers, Ben made his first move, but Kevin used the pie rule to choose whether to stick with the move or change sides.

sense.definition: a straight line of spaces running horizontally across the game board at a right angle to a column
(in backgammon) to safely bring one's pieces home and out of the game

examples.x: After a strategic move, he bore off the pieces, ending the game.
The key to winning is efficiently bearing off the checkers.
The champion player knew how to bear off the pieces efficiently.

sense.definition: a player who switches allegiances or teams, typically to gain an advantage or for strategic reasons
a central reserve or stockpile of unused tiles, cards, or pieces that players can draw from during the game when they need additional resources or replacements

examples.x: She drew a tile from the boneyard when she couldn't make a move with the pieces in her hand.
The players continued to take turns, hoping for a better card from the boneyard.
He was lucky to pull a matching domino from the boneyard and keep his turn going.
When no more moves were possible, they all started drawing from the boneyard.

sense.definition: a chain reaction where players set up a line of dominoes to knock one another down in a visually pleasing way

examples.x: He spent hours setting up the domino toppling display, only to watch it fall in seconds.
She carefully arranged the pieces for a domino toppling routine that would take several minutes to complete.
His favorite hobby is domino toppling, and he often shares videos of his creations online.
They decided to create a giant domino toppling course that twisted and turned across the whole room.
